title: Colony PCR with ready-to-load PCR master mix (similar to GoTaq)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1rv6p","text":"This protocol makes a master mix for PCR that is ready to load into an agarose gel. Useful for colony PCR and related.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: First make these: 40% sucrose with 0.25% xylene cyanol and 0.25% orange G (this is a 5X stock) To make 10 ml: 4g sucrose 25 mg xylene cyanol 25 mg orange G bring to 10 ml with ddH2O Master mix ingredients for 2X: %s %s It can also be useful to make a 1.25X master mix where you only need to add primers and no water, for colony PCR. For 1.25X master mix: %s %s Aliquot to 1ml or less and store aliquots at -20 °C.

Prepare forward and reverse primers at 2.5 micromolar each in water. Design primers to amplify across the region being tested - typically looking for an insertion by the length of the PCR products. e.g. 1 ml primer mix 25 ul forward primer (100 uM) 25 ul reverse primer (100 uM) 950 ul water
Combine 2 volumes of 2.5 micromolar primer mix with 8 volumes of 1.25X master mix from above for example, add 200 ul primer mix to 800 ul master mix
Put 10 ul of PCR master mix with primers into as many PCR tubes as needed
Mark the colonies you would like to do PCR from by circling and numbering the plate. Colonies must be far enough apart from others that you are able to pick a single colony.

Put a tip on a 10 microliter pipet set at 7 microliters and touch the tip to a marked colony. Move the tip into the PCR tube and pipet up and down at least 5 times while swirling the tip to move the colony into the PCR tube
After picking all colonies, run a standard PCR with an elongation time sufficient to extend across your amplicon and load directly onto a gel.
creator: Stephen Floor
